subject: Chemistry
title: Nicolas Flamel: The Legendary Goldmaker
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Nicolas Flamel: The Legendary Goldmaker.

hook.text: Nicolas Flamel was a real 14th-century Parisian bookseller who supposedly discovered how to make gold.

buildup.text: Legend says he bought a mysterious book by 'Abraham the Jew' containing the secret of transmutation.

discovery.text: After studying it for 21 years, he allegedly transmuted mercury into gold on January 17, 1382.

twist.text: He really did become enormously wealthy—but historians believe he got rich from real estate, not alchemy.

climax.text: J.K. Rowling put Flamel in Harry Potter as the maker of the philosopher's stone. He's fiction's favorite alchemist.

punchline.text: A bookseller turned millionaire. The legend says gold; history says real estate.

quiz.question: How did the real Nicolas Flamel most likely get wealthy?
options: Through real estate investments, not alchemical transmutation
By selling fake gold to the French court
By writing bestselling books about alchemy
